summary: The ideal gas law ties together the pressure, volume, amount and temperature of a gas: P·V = n·R·T. It is the workhorse equation for gas problems.
method: List the four state variables — pressure P, volume V, amount n, temperature T — and mark the unknown.
Convert temperature to kelvin (T[K] = T[°C] + 273.15); the law is wrong with Celsius.
Use SI units: pascals, cubic metres, moles, kelvin (R is already in SI).
Rearrange P·V = n·R·T for the unknown.
Substitute and compute; the calculator supplies R ≈ 8.314 J/(mol·K).

example.title: Pressure of gas in a tank
steps: The unknown is P, so use P = n·R·T / V.
Compute the top: 2 · 8.314 · 300 ≈ 4988, then divide by 0.05.
